title: Three Protesters Killed as Crackdown Intensifies
detail: Security forces killed three protesters on the third day of nationwide unrest: Amirhesam Khodayarifard in Kuhdasht (Lorestan), Dariush Ansari Bakhtiariwand in Fuladshahr (Isfahan), and Khodadad Shirvani in Marvdasht (Fars). Witnesses reported live ammunition used against demonstrators. Human rights organizations called for urgent international intervention.

title: Severe Confrontations in Tehran, Isfahan, Shiraz, Kermanshah
detail: Major clashes erupted between protesters and security forces in at least four major cities. In Tehran, demonstrators set fire to banks and government buildings near Enghelab Square. In Isfahan, crowds blocked the Si-o-se-pol bridge. Kermanshah saw running battles in bazaar districts. The IRGC deployed anti-riot units with armored vehicles to multiple provincial capitals.

title: Internet Throttled Across Major Cities
detail: Internet monitoring groups reported severe bandwidth throttling across Iran's major cities, with mobile data speeds dropping to near-unusable levels. Instagram and WhatsApp were completely blocked. NetBlocks recorded connectivity at approximately 15% of normal levels in Tehran and below 10% in protest hotspots like Kermanshah and Lorestan.
